source_jobs.title: Assembly Technician I
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

We are seeking an Assembly Technician I to join a nimble, fast-paced team at the front lines of our factory, building and testing industrial control panels. You will have the opportunity to work in a variety of different operations from enclosure modification through wiring through final testing. As Podium iterates and implements new technologies, you will work hand-in-hand with the engineering team to both operate and test the new methods and equipment, while providing feedback and suggestions to help continually improve our production process.



What You’ll Do

 - Modify enclosures to include cutouts for panel mounted devices.

 - Retrieve components from stockroom and help maintain stockroom inventory levels

 - Mount components to the panel and wire them together bsaed on work instructions

 - Complete inspection procedures to ensure panels were assembled correctly

 - Assist with preparing completed panels for shipping, and helping load/unload goods from trucks when needed.

 - Test new methods and operate prototype automated equipment to help improve the speed and quality of the panels Podium builds, and provide constructive and detailed feedback.



Who You Are

There are 4 values that all Podium employees are expected to demonstrate (albeit imperfectly):

 - Win for the customer. We sit on the same side of the table as our customers - their wins are our wins. We’re building the vendor we always wished we had.

 - Default to transparency. We believe in honesty, transparency, and kindness. It’s about how you get things done and not just what gets done.

 - Speed is a feature. We move fast and take ownership. Don’t let perfect be the enemy of good.

 - Win and lose as a team. Individuals drive inputs, teams drive outputs. We celebrate success together but aren’t afraid to fail and iterate. We own outcomes together and don’t deflect blame.



Required for this role

 - High school diploma or equivalent

 - Mechanically inclined and experience doing light sheet metal modification work

 - Attention to detail in following written instructions

 - Understanding of electrical fundamentals (AC vs DC voltage, function of overcurrent protection devices, sizing of wiring)

 - Proficient in Microsoft Office

 - Able to work on your feet 8 hours/day

 - Able to lift items that are 25 lbs

 - Available to work in-person in Brooklyn, NY Monday through Friday.



Preferred for this role

 - Experience working in a UL 508A panel shop or with industrial control panels

 - Familiar with DTM, E3, EPLAN, or AutoCAD Electrical

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

We're hiring a Sales Engineer to join our commercial team and serve as the technical backbone of our sales motion. You'll be the first technical voice customers hear, translating their electrical specifications, single-line diagrams, and project requirements into accurate quotes and clear proposals. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of engineering and commercial teams, where the speed and quality of your work directly shapes how quickly we can deliver critical infrastructure to our customers.

You'll work closely with our sales, engineering, and operations teams to scope projects, run discovery calls, develop quotes, and shepherd opportunities from first contact through handoff to production. As one of the first members of our commercial team, you'll also help define the playbooks, tooling, and processes that scale with us.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead first calls with prospective customers to understand their control panel needs, project timelines, and technical requirements

 - Review customer-provided documentation (single-line diagrams, BOMs, specifications, RFQs) and translate it into standard inputs that drive our quoting process

 - Partner with our engineering team to scope complex or non-standard builds

 - Own the quote-to-close technical workflow, including follow-ups, clarifications, and revisions

 - Build and refine internal software tooling and processes that make our quoting motion faster and more accurate

 - Serve as the technical bridge between customers and our internal design and manufacturing teams

 - Contribute customer insights back to product and engineering to inform our roadmap


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years in a sales engineering, applications engineering, or technical pre-sales role, ideally in industrial automation, electrical equipment, power distribution, or related fields

 - Strong working knowledge of industrial control panels, motor controls, PLCs, VFDs, or related electrical equipment. You can read a single-line diagram and ask the right questions

 - Excellent written and verbal communication, you can explain technical tradeoffs to both engineers and non-technical buyers

 - Customer-obsessed mindset with a track record of building trust with technical buyers

Nice to Have

 - Background in electrical engineering, mechatronics, or a related technical discipline

 - Experience selling into industrial end markets (data centers, utilities, manufacturing, energy, water/wastewater)

 - Familiarity with UL 508A standards

source_jobs.title: Electrical Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

We are seeking a full-time Electrical Design Engineer to work across control panel design, build, and test. You will have the opportunity to work across the end-to-end process in a variety of operations from electrical design to enclosure modification through final testing.



What You’ll Do

 - Translate customer design requirements into build-ready UL 508A compliant electrical schematics

 - Build out Podium’s component database and standardized symbol library

 - Test, implement, and provide feedback on new software tools that help speed up the schematic creation process

 - Support our operations in answering technician questions and turning those questions into process improvement sprints

 - Support the automation of the panel build process through testing of new methods and tools, and ensuring necessary information is digitized during the design phase.



Who You Are

There are 4 values that all Podium employees are expected to demonstrate (albeit imperfectly):

 - Customer Obsession: We sit on the same side of the table as our customers and build things to solve their biggest problems

 - High Integrity: We strive to be honest, transparent, and kind. We do the right thing even when it's not the easy or obvious path, and believe that how things get done is just as important as what gets done.

 - Have Grit: We pick up the broom and have a bias for action. We enjoy doing things outside of our comfort zone, and we roll up our sleeves and take on those challenges head-on.

 - Win and Lose as a Team: We work collaboratively and take primary fulfillment from the team’s success. While we certainly celebrate individual achievements, we ultimately win as a team.



Required for this role

 - Must have completed a 4-year degree in Electrical Engineering, Systems Engineering or a related field

 - 0-2 years of work experience in electrical engineering with good understanding of electrical fundamentals (AC vs DC voltage, function of overcurrent protection devices, sizing of wiring)

 - Familiar with DTM, E3, EPLAN, or AutoCAD Electrical

 - Available to work in-person in Brooklyn, NY (in person, 5 days/week)



Nice to have

 - Experience working in a UL 508A panel shop or designing industrial control panels

 - Mechanically inclined and experience doing light sheet metal modification work and wiring



Additional Information

 - Sponsorship for US employment authorization is not available now or in the future for this position.

 - Relocation assistance is not available for this position.

 - Compensation of $70,000 - $100,000 commensurate with experience, with competitive equity package

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter & People Partner
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

We're looking for our first dedicated Technical Recruiter & People Partner to own how we attract, hire, and take care of the people building Podium. You'll run full-cycle recruiting across engineering, production, and business operations, and you'll also own the experience employees have from offer letter through their first months on the team. This is a rare opportunity to shape both the talent and people functions at a hardware startup company from the ground up.

You'll source creatively, close hard, and bring rigor to hiring. You'll also build the systems and rituals that make Podium a place where great people do their best work - clean onboarding, smooth employee setup, thoughtful people programs, and a culture that reflects how we operate.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Recruiting

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for technical roles across software, hardware, controls, manufacturing, and operations

 - Partner with the founders and hiring managers to scope roles, build hiring plans, and define interview loops that actually predict performance

 - Source proactively. Most of our best hires won't be applying through a job board; you'll find them, engage them, and bring them in

 - Run a fast, high-signal candidate experience that reflects how we operate as a company

 - Build the operating system for talent at Podium: ATS hygiene, scorecards, structured interviews, referral programs, and reporting


PEOPLE PARTNER

 - Own onboarding end-to-end — from offer acceptance through ramp — so every new hire shows up to a great first day and a clear path to contribution

 - Manage employee setup: equipment, accounts, payroll and benefits enrollment, I-9s, and the rest of the operational details that make day one feel seamless

 - Design and run people programs that strengthen the team: onboarding journeys, recognition, team rituals, feedback cycles, and culture-building moments

 - Partner with leadership on policies, handbook, compliance, and HR operations as the company scales

 - Help shape employer brand, candidate marketing, and our presence in the hardware and tech talent communities


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years of experience spanning technical recruiting and people operations, ideally in-house at a high-growth startup

 - Full-cycle recruiting track record across diverse technical functions, inlcuding hardware, electrical, mechanical, controls, or manufacturing talent

 - Hands-on experience with onboarding, employee setup, HRIS/payroll tools, and the operational backbone of a people function

 - Excellent written and verbal communication - you can credibly represent a technical company to technical candidates and serve as a steady point of contact for employees

 - High ownership, low ego, fast metabolism for work; comfortable operating with ambiguity and minimal process

 - Genuine interest in what we're building. You don't need an engineering background, but you do need to be curious about how things get made

Additional Information

 - Sponsorship for US employment authorization is not available now or in the future for this position.

 - Relocation assistance is not available for this position.

 - Compensation of $100,000 - $130,000 commensurate with experience, with competitive equity package

source_jobs.title: Head of Operations
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

As Head of Operations, you will own everything that happens across panel design, fabrication, and delivery. This is a senior leadership role reporting directly to the Co-Founder/CEO, responsible for building and running the operational backbone of a fast-scaling hardware company. You will oversee production across our current and future manufacturing facilities, lead our supply chain and procurement function, and drive site selection and expansion as we grow our footprint.

This role is equal parts builder and operator - you'll set strategy and get into the details. The right person has deep experience in manufacturing/production environments and thrives at the intersection of process rigor, people leadership, and scrappy problem-solving.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

Production & Manufacturing

 - Own end-to-end production operations across all current and future Podium facilities, with full accountability for output, quality, and throughput

 - Build and continuously improve manufacturing processes, workflows, and quality systems as we scale from early production to high-volume operations

 - Recruit, develop, and lead a world-class operations team including facility general managers, production supervisors, and technicians

 - Partner with engineering to drive DFM (design for manufacturability) and ensure a tight feedback loop between product and production

Supply Chain & Procurement

 - Lead all procurement and sourcing for components, materials, and equipment — managing vendor relationships from selection through ongoing performance

 - Drive strategic vendor negotiations to optimize cost, lead time, and supply reliability as our volumes grow

 - Build resilient supply chain systems that can absorb disruptions and support rapid product iteration

 - Develop and manage inventory strategies, demand planning, and materials forecasting

Facilities & Expansion

 - Own site selection and buildout for new manufacturing facilities as Podium expands geographically

 - Develop the criteria, process, and execution plan for evaluating and standing up new production sites

 - Manage relationships with landlords, contractors, and local stakeholders across all facilities

 - Ensure all sites meet safety, compliance, and operational standards




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 10+ years of progressive operations leadership experience in manufacturing, industrial equipment, or related sectors

 - Demonstrated track record scaling manufacturing operations — you've taken a facility or production line from early-stage to meaningful volume

 - Deep expertise in supply chain management, strategic sourcing, and vendor negotiation in a hardware context

 - Experience selecting and standing up new manufacturing facilities or sites

 - Strong people leader who can build teams, develop talent, and operate effectively across hourly and salaried workforces

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity and a startup environment — you're able to define the playbook, not just execute one

 - Analytical and data-driven; able to define and track operational KPIs and make decisions grounded in metrics

 - Excellent cross-functional collaborator with engineering, finance, and leadership

 - Background in lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, or other continuous improvement methodologies

Nice to Have

 - Experience in electrical equipment, industrial automation, or control systems manufacturing

 - Experience with ERP (Odoo) or MES systems in a manufacturing context

source_jobs.title: Supply Chain Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.

We're hiring a Supply Chain Operations Lead to own procurement and make on-time delivery the default at Podium. Speed is our core product promise — we commit to 3–4 week lead times for control panels, which leaves 1–2 weeks to source every component a build requires. Procurement isn't a back-office function here; it's the engine that determines whether we keep that promise. You'll convert requirements into purchase orders, expedite at-risk items, and drive Okay-to-Build readiness so production never waits on parts.

This role goes well beyond purchasing. You'll be the primary operator of Purchasing in Odoo, our ERP, along with our recently adopted AI supply chain platform. The right candidate will not just run these systems, but shape them. You'll act as an internal product owner for our supply chain stack.

You'll partner closely with our electrical engineering, product, technical sales, operations, and finance teams to keep every project fully tracked and accurately costed. You'll own the supply base directly — building relationships with inside sales reps and technical contacts across our vendors. As one of our first dedicated supply chain hires, you'll define the sourcing playbook, tooling, and supplier strategy that scale with the company.


What You'll Do

 - Own end-to-end purchasing for every active project —sourcing and issuing POs to meet hardware budgets and lead-time requirements

 - Forecast component demand across the active project pipeline and get ahead of long-lead items so the 1–2 week procurement window is never the constraint

 - Project-manage the BOM across all active builds — tracking every line from PO to receipt, expediting at-risk items, and driving Okay-to-Build readiness so production never stalls

 - Qualify and onboard new suppliers, negotiate pricing and payment terms, and partner with finance to hit hardware savings targets — continuously scoring vendors on price, lead time, quality, and communication

 - Define and own procurement KPIs — on-time delivery, PO cycle time, and cost variance — and report them to leadership

 - Act as internal product owner for our supply chain systems: identifying process improvements and increasing data accuracy in partnership with our product team

 - Own component continuity — approving alternates, securing multi-source and distributor access on critical parts, and monitoring EOL/change notices

 - Establish the procurement playbook from the ground up — sourcing strategy, supplier scorecards, and the processes that scale as we grow

 - Own relationships directly with inside sales reps and technical contacts across our vendor base, traveling to meet them and represent Podium at conferences (1-2 per quarter)
   

What We're Looking For

 - 1-3 years in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain within a hardware, manufacturing, or electrical/industrial environment

 - Proficiency with an ERP/MRP system and strong data and business-systems instincts — you're comfortable living in software day to day

 - Strong project management skills and the ability to keep many moving parts on schedule in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - A builder mindset towards tools and process; empowered to drive improvements

 - Excellent communication and relationship-building skills with suppliers: you can be friendly while simultaneously firm on what Podium needs to grow

 - This role is on-site 5 days / week at our Brooklyn, NY HQ



Nice to Have

 - Experience sourcing electrical or electromechanical components — contr

source_jobs.title: Office & Facilities Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Podium Automation http://www.podiumautomation.com/ is reimagining industrial control panel manufacturing through software-enabled design and automated fabrication. We're building technology to create a new industrial supply chain - reducing lead times for critical electrical equipment from months to days. We've raised over $23M from top-tier investors including Construct Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Transition Ventures, Sunflower Capital, Banter Capital, and SV Angel.



About the Role

We're hiring an Office & Facilities Coordinator to keep our Brooklyn facility running, take care of the team, and quietly handle the hundred things that would otherwise pull engineers and operators off their work. You'll own the physical space at 300 Meserole, the experience of working here day to day, and the logistics behind getting our team in front of customers at tradeshows. This is a hands-on, in-person role for someone who likes to be the person who gets things done.



What You'll Do

People Experience

 - Run the rhythms that make this a great place to work: Wednesday team lunches, birthdays and celebrations, holiday and summer parties

 - Assist with new-hire setup end to end — desks, chairs, laptops, monitors, and account provisioning (Gmail, Slack, Brivo) so people are productive on day one

 - Build and run visitor management: a sign-in system with a short-form NDA, food when needed, and lightweight event support

Facilities

 - Be the point person with our landlord and the building super on the basics: bathrooms and plumbing, annual fire extinguisher service, and AC/heating maintenance

 - Manage building vendors, including cleaner coordination and waste management

 - Own facilities compliance, including fire drills, first aid supplies and training

 - Handle the unglamorous-but-critical: laundering arc flash suits, patio maintenance, coordinating electricians and building improvements, and dealing with the city or contractors when they dig a hole in front of our loading door (true story)

Office & Warehouse Stocking

 - Keep the office and warehouse stocked — snacks, coffee, paper goods, etc.

 - Keep the space organized, lead facility rebranding, and solve ad-hoc issues from time to time

Tradeshow Support & Coordination

 - Order and manage swag, merch, and booth materials; handle shipping to and from events

 - Coordinate hotel blocks and travel schedules for the team

 - Maintain a master calendar for each event

What We're Looking For

 - 3+ years in office management, facilities, workplace operations, or a similar high-ownership operations role

 - A bias toward action: you see what needs doing and handle it without being asked

 - Strong vendor and stakeholder management; comfortable being the face to landlords, contractors, and the city

 - Highly organized, with the judgment to juggle competing priorities and the follow-through to close loops

 - Comfortable in a physical environment — a manufacturing floor, not just a desk

 - Warm, people-first instincts; you care about how it feels to work here

Nice to Have

 - Experience in a startup, manufacturing, or warehouse environment

 - Familiarity with tools like Brivo, Slack, Google Workspace, or Odoo

 - First aid / OSHA awareness or willingness to get certified

notes: Industrial control panel manufacturing startup with software-enabled design and automated fabrication
